title: <div>Here are some great guidelines when you are starting a free-write warm-up e
content:
Here are some great guidelines when you are starting a free-write warm-up exercise:
  1. Keep your fingers moving on the keyboard for 5 minutes. 
  2. Write everything that comes to mind, even seemingly unrelated comments like, "I hate writing. This is too hard. I don't think I would like eating squid if I had the chance."
  3. Don't self-edit. Get it all down without worrying about how it looks or whether or not it is correct.
  4. Be outrageous. Use vocabulary and descriptions that sound overboard, silly, or absurd. Make comparisons and connections to other subjects (even if they seem at first glance to be irrelevant or unrelated).
  5. Keep writing, no matter what, until your first 5 minutes are up, and then stop.

Instructions
Perform the following tasks:
On a Word document or the text entry box:
Think about writing. What do you feel when you think about writing an essay? What do you imagine when you think about writing an essay? Has there ever been a piece of written language that has influenced you in some way? What was it, and why? Do you think something you write could ever affect someone else similarly?
